page: Amazon Web Services m8id.32xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). The system is configured with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The combination of high-speed DDR5 memory, massive local NVMe storage, and dedicated CPU allocation provides a robust foundation for data-intensive operations.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple domains. Multi-core CPU performance is highly rated, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 138,373.51 and a Passmark CPU score of 122,861.45, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also exceptional, reaching 392.68 GB/sec for large block read-only operations. Database performance is strong, with Redis SET operations achieving 210.58 million operations per second. However, single-core CPU performance is more moderate, scoring 2,119.88 in stress-ng single-core tests, placing it in the middle 50%.

Given its high resource density, including 512 GB of DDR5 RAM and 7.6 TB of local NVMe SSDs, the m8id.32xlarge offers a balanced cost-to-performance profile for enterprise workloads that require high memory bandwidth and fast local storage without the need for dedicated GPUs. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks, such as prompt processing using 7B and 70B models.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 210.58 million operations per second. While single-core performance is average, its massive multi-threaded capacity and high-speed local storage make it highly efficient for demanding workloads. The m8id.32xlarge is optimized for large-scale databases, software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose instance combining 128 vCPUs, DDR5 memory, and 7.6 TB of local NVMe storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.

Equipped with 512 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 50 Gbps network bandwidth
Includes 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes
